behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a handoff failure between request-construction code and API-client wiring, not just isolated parsing logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a stub client that captures outbound payload is sufficient; real network/OS is not required. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about request contents before model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a fake in-process provider client, invoke classify() with a known taxonomy, and assert the outbound request payload contains the full taxonomy label list exactly once and untruncated.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local normalization/validation logic (unexpected label -> fallback). No required module-boundary effect beyond a single response value transformation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network dependence. LLM-DEP: no dependence on real model quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on deterministic mapping rule for one returned label.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/response-handling function with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw label "medical"; assert result is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: explicitly scoped to normalization determinism for identical input, which is pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none (input fixed). REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration+ because behavior text constrains to normalization step with identical raw responses.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/spacing quirks) and assert output label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI calls; correctness depends on what the real model/provider returns over time. A stub would hide nondeterministic provider behavior and cannot validate the conditional 'unless provider response changes.' MULTI-STEP: repeated calls but not a chained multi-agent pipeline, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration/System because behavior text says "live API calls" and consistency relative to real provider responses.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls for the same document+taxonomy, log raw provider label and normalized final label each run, and assert final-label changes only occur on runs where raw provider response changed.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is policy enforcement at module handoff (token counting + dispatch decision), best caught by wiring request builder with tokenizer/config and fake client call tracking. REAL-INFRA: no real network needed. LLM-DEP: no model-output dependence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior is pre-dispatch budget enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget, pass an over-budget document, and assert module either truncates or rejects per policy and only dispatches when post-policy token count is within budget.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: provider-reported usage is an external behavior; stubs can hide tokenizer/accounting mismatches. LLM-DEP: execution relies on a real AI API response metadata path, and the assertion is about real model/provider token accounting. MULTI-STEP: single call, not workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires "provider-reported token usage" (real provider signal), not local estimates only.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative 'typical' documents and assert provider-reported prompt/completion/total tokens are <= configured per-call budget (or policy-acceptable thresholds).

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is retry orchestration across modules (error handling + backoff + retry). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process fake provider returning rate-limit then success; real network not required for minimum boundary. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality/content. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is protocol-level retry logic, testable with deterministic stubbed responses.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider that returns rate-limit on first call and success on second; assert classifier delays per retry policy, retries once (or per config), and returns successful label instead of surfacing rate-limit error.
